properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 39: Investment Property Then Main Residence**

Property purchased as investment, rented, then converted to main residence. Cannot use six-year absence rule.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Sep 2015 to 30 Jun 2019: Rental/Investment (3.83 years) - taxable
- 30 Jun 2019 to 15 Nov 2024: Main residence (5.38 years) - exempt
- Total ownership: 9.21 years

**Key Calculations:**
- Total capital gain: $462,650
- Time apportionment:
  - Investment period: 3.83/9.21 years = 41.6% taxable
  - Main residence period: 5.38/9.21 years = 58.4% exempt
- Six-year absence rule DOES NOT apply (property must be main residence before renting)
- Taxable capital gain: $462,650 × 41.6% = $192,462
- After 50% CGT discount: $192,462 × 50% = $96,231
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $96,231**

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $96,231
- 41.6% of ownership period is taxable (investment period)

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-185 ITAA 1997 (Partial Main Residence Exemption)
- Section 118-145 ITAA 1997 (Six-Year Rule - NOT applicable)
- Six-year absence rule only applies if property was main residence BEFORE being rented
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount)
- Time apportionment method
